desc: Zakopane is Poland's Tatra resort town: a mountain base for ski season, alpine hiking, wooden highlander culture, and dramatic views year-round.
hls: Strong winter ski and snow scene
Thermal baths nearby for shoulder months

todo.name: Krupówki & Zakopane Style walk
todo.desc: Stroll Krupówki, sample oscypek, and spot the carved timber villas of the Zakopane architectural style.

todo.name: Gubałówka viewpoint
todo.desc: Ride the funicular for a quick panorama over Zakopane and the Tatra ridge, especially photogenic near sunset.

todo.name: Kasprowy Wierch cable car
todo.desc: Go up for alpine terrain: skiing in winter or high-altitude trail access in warmer months.

todo.name: Morskie Oko trail day
todo.desc: Plan an early start to one of the region's classic mountain lake walks inside Tatra National Park.

todo.name: Thermal spa evening
todo.desc: Use nearby thermal pools to recover after ski or hiking days, ideal in cold weather.

todo.name: Chochołów or Podhale villages
todo.desc: Visit traditional highlander villages for wooden cottages, folk crafts, and regional cuisine.

wifi.note: Good in hotels and central cafés; mountain huts and outlying valleys can be patchy.

prac.fltNote: No commercial airport in town; fly to Kraków (or occasionally Poprad) and continue by road/rail to Zakopane.

airports.name: Kraków John Paul II International Airport

prac.visa: Poland is in Schengen; standard Schengen entry rules apply.
alerts: Book early for Christmas/New Year and school-holiday ski weeks.
Mountain weather changes quickly; check avalanche and trail notices before heading out.

sweet: June to September suits hiking and outdoor activities, while winter is best for snow sports.
